events.text: {{characters.alea.name}} looked at {{characters.lyra.name}}. {{characters.lyra.name}} didn't look up. Her hands gripped her knees tightly.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: It's my fault. If I hadn't opened that door, they wouldn't have——if I'd listened to {{characters.soren.name}}, if I hadn't——

events.speaker: {{characters.alea.name}}
events.text: {{characters.lyra.name}}.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: That child, I helped her up, I said take me inside, I said we'd get out together, and then——

events.speaker: {{characters.alea.name}}
events.text: {{characters.lyra.name}}.
She finally stopped. She looked up at {{characters.alea.name}}.

{{characters.alea.name}} looked at her with no moral platitudes, no words about whose fault it was or wasn't. She simply looked at her, directly and unadorned, taking in the sight of her as she was in this moment.
Then she knelt on one knee before {{characters.lyra.name}}.

{{characters.lyra.name}} froze. She'd never seen {{characters.alea.name}} do that before. The one who always stood in front, always walked ahead first, the one who was always first to take that step——was now on one knee in front of her, lowering her eyes to match hers.
{{characters.alea.name}} reached out and touched her head. The touch was light, as if confirming something was still there.
Then she pulled {{characters.lyra.name}} into an embrace. Not a comforting pat, but a real hold, wrapping her whole body——trying to shrink itself——inside.
{{characters.lyra.name}} couldn't cry. She didn't have the strength left. She just leaned into that embrace, resting against something steadier than herself, pausing for just one second.
Then she felt something. {{characters.alea.name}}'s hand, searching for something. She suddenly felt pressure against her left neck——

Before {{characters.lyra.name}} could react, an emergency neural synchronizer had already completed injection. The liquid entering her neural circuit felt like immediate clarity being pulled back from the edges of numbness. Her sync rate stabilized rapidly over the next few seconds.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: {{characters.alea.name}}——
But {{characters.alea.name}} had already released her, stepping back. She pulled the remaining emergency synchronizer from her pocket and pressed it into {{characters.lyra.name}}'s hand.
Then came something else.

A small metal tag, its edges worn smooth with age, hanging from a thin chain. The metal surface bore several fine scratches from long use. On the front was engraved a sequence of numbers and letters——a soul's serial number.
It was hers. It was {{characters.alea.name}}'s.
Her life nameplate.
{{characters.lyra.name}} held the nameplate and looked up.

events.speaker: {{characters.lyra.name}}
events.text: This is——
{{characters.alea.name}} didn't let her finish. She leaned in close to {{characters.lyra.name}}'s ear, her voice so soft only one person in that corridor could hear what she said.

events.speaker: {{characters.alea.name}}
events.text: <whisper>Goodbye. You have to be well.</whisper>
Then she stood up.

Her standing was natural, like rising from a cot every morning. She straightened, turned around, and looked at {{characters.soren.name}} and {{characters.elias.name}}. Both were watching her. Something outside calculation lived in {{characters.soren.name}}'s eyes. {{characters.elias.name}}'s lips moved, but no sound came out.
Her face carried no sadness, no fear, no unbearable grandeur. Only calm, like someone who had finished thinking through everything and now had only one thing left to do.
